issues.detail: The test requires “no partial state is observable between them,” but REQ-031 states only that “one action hides the project from every public surface.” It does not require atomic visibility changes across the platform and external repository.
issues.fix: Remove the atomicity assertion. After one founder action completes, assert that every applicable public surface and the existing repository are hidden.

issues.detail: “Given the v1 moderation surface, When probed” defines neither a bounded surface inventory nor an observable probe, so the global assertion that no automated takedown surface or pipeline exists cannot be judged deterministically.
issues.fix: Specify concrete observables, such as the API/route and scheduled-job registries containing no automated takedown entry, plus a controlled report-like event causing no automatic visibility change.

issues.detail: REQ-031 explicitly requires that follow-up “return to the NGO, cancel, or un-hide” use normal paths. AT-031.06 exercises only cancellation and un-hide; the return-to-NGO path has no test, despite the coverage map claiming the clause is covered.
issues.fix: Add a hidden-project fixture that exercises the ordinary return-to-NGO path and asserts its normal outcome without a special hidden lifecycle state or transition.

issues.detail: The coverage row maps “Visibility switch, never a lifecycle transition” to AT-031.05, but that test compares lifecycle state only around hide. For un-hide it asserts merely that no lifecycle event is emitted, so an un-hide that silently changes lifecycle state could pass.
issues.fix: Read and compare lifecycle state immediately before and after both hide and un-hide, while retaining the transition-event assertion.
